claim: Durable execution ensures code executes exactly once in the presence of failures by transparently managing state and resurrecting execution on different hosts without developer intervention.
actionable: Use durable execution frameworks to build stateful workflows that recover from failures automatically without custom error handling.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Long-running agents require durable execution and state management more than interactive agents because they operate asynchronously across distributed systems where failures are common.
actionable: Implement durable RPC and state persistence when building agents that run for extended periods without continuous human prompting.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Execution histories provide complete audit trails and observability into agent operations, which is valuable for debugging, model improvement, and compliance.
actionable: Log and analyze complete execution traces to understand agent behavior, identify failure patterns, and improve agent performance iteratively.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Context engineering at scale requires pulling real-time data from diverse sources (APIs, Slack messages, documents) and processing it efficiently before passing to agents.
actionable: Build orchestration layers to aggregate and transform context from multiple sources in real-time rather than relying on static embeddings.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Specialized agents focused on specific tasks are more effective than monolithic agents when building multi-agent systems because they allow for task-specific optimization and delegation.
actionable: Decompose complex agent workflows into specialized agents (reservation agents, validator agents, etc.) that can be composed together.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Durable RPC protocols need to support asynchronous tool invocations that may take hours or days to complete, unlike standard synchronous request-response patterns.
actionable: Design agent APIs to handle async tool calls with callbacks or polling mechanisms rather than blocking on immediate responses.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Business processes with well-defined sequences should use deterministic orchestration with audit trails rather than nondeterministic agents to maintain system of record.
actionable: Use durable execution workflows for critical business processes that require strong guarantees; reserve agents for exploratory or discretionary tasks.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Event sourcing enables time-travel debugging and bug recovery by replaying execution with corrected code, restoring corrupted state without manual remediation.
actionable: Implement event-sourced workflow execution to enable replaying workflows from a known good state with patched logic.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Large-scale agent deployments require multi-region failover and business continuity features to handle regional outages with minimal disruption.
actionable: Configure multi-region namespace support and automatic failover to maintain availability during cloud provider outages.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Coding agents represent a shift from code completion to full application generation, where agents work in background on long-running tasks without human prompting between steps.
actionable: Design coding agents for background execution with checkpoints rather than synchronous, interactive flows.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: Observability becomes critical for agents because they are nondeterministic; you need full visibility into what the agent did, why it failed, and what state it left behind.
actionable: Instrument agents to expose tool calls, reasoning steps, and state changes for debugging and monitoring.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z

claim: APIs and data relationships will become more valuable than the ability to build applications from scratch as agents commoditize app generation.
actionable: Invest in data aggregation, relationship management, and API quality rather than trying to compete on app-building capability.
source_title: Temporal CEO on AI Agents & The Future of Software | Deep Dives with a16z
